content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 2.2 WARN — release notes explicitly call the build a "public beta" and say "We're still testing… expect rough edges" (fastlane/metadata/en-US/release_notes.txt:1-2), which may trigger review questions.
Pierre: Apple's guideline 2.2 rejects apps presented as beta, demo, or trial versions — the App Store is only for finished builds, and "public beta" plus "expect rough edges" reads as an unfinished product. The description itself is clean, but reviewers do read release notes. Rewrite the notes to describe the new sync feature positively (e.g., "New: task sync across devices") and remove all beta/testing language, or distribute the beta via TestFlight instead.
